Halter · P5 Collar

Humidity Ingress Model

Physics-based simulation predicting moisture build-up inside the P5 collar over its 6-year service life. Used to decide overmould material, desiccant configuration, and bung sealing strategy.

01

Click any dot on the collar to see what is and isn't modelled for that component, with source attribution.

02

How the model steps through each minute

Eight computational steps repeat every minute of simulated time, over the 6-year ERA5 weather history.
1Read weather
External T & RH from ERA5

Hourly air temperature and relative humidity from Open-Meteo ERA5 reanalysis for the selected location, interpolated to per-minute resolution.

2Compute vapour pressure
External vapour pressure eext

Magnus-Tetens formula above 0 °C; ice form (WMO 2008) below 0 °C. Gives the outside vapour partial pressure that drives transport across the walls.

3Wall transport
Vapour through external walls

Two parallel external paths:

  • TPU overmould → bridge cavity
  • PC tub walls → Cue / Main tubs (direct)

P(T) × Δp with Arrhenius temperature scaling, multiplied by a freeze_factor that halts transport between 0 → −30 °C.

4Wall sorption
Bulk polymer absorbs water

Polymer walls store water in their bulk via 2-node finite-difference Fickian diffusion. Each wall has an outer and inner node tracking absorbed water — slow equilibration buffers the cavity from quick external swings.

5Zone balance
Per-zone vapour + bridge↔tub coupling

Three cavities (TPU bridge, Cue, Main) track vapour mass independently. Bridge↔tub coupling via the bung has two parallel sub-paths:

  • Air-hole: free vapour diffusion (Fick)
  • Solid TPU body: polymer permeation

The sealed-bung variant disables both, decoupling bridge from tubs entirely.

6Desiccant
Silica gel exchanges water with cavity vapour

If gel is present, each zone's gel pack adsorbs/desorbs water with cavity vapour using a hysteresis isotherm + linear-driving-force kinetics. Capacity falls as temperature rises.

7Condensation check
Liquid (T ≥ 0 °C) or frost (T < 0 °C)

If cavity vapour exceeds saturation at the cavity temperature, the excess deposits onto cavity surfaces — as liquid water at warm T, as frost below 0 °C. Re-evaporates on the next warm period.

8Record state
Per-zone RH, wall sorption, gel state, events

Save the current state of every tracked variable. After 6 years (3.15 million minutes) the model returns a full time-series plus summary metrics — feeding the comparison views in this dashboard.
